subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, EC, Eurostat, WJP, RSF, TI, WHO, EEA) + Slovenian official (SURS, Bank of Slovenia, IMAD) + independent press (Balkan Insight, EUobserver).
A small, wealthy, stable EU/eurozone democracy with strong institutions; 2026 brought an orderly, lawful change of government with a democratic-backsliding watch under the returning right.

Standout features: strong institutions, sound banks, very low crime, against ageing demographics, German-supply-chain export concentration, and recurrent catastrophic flooding. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone on political-narrative items.

economic.notes: A high-complexity export economy, sound banks, and euro membership, with German-supply-chain exposure the risk.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~66% GDP on a downward path with a ~2.8% deficit target, the IMF urging consolidation. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Pooled at the ECB - no own policy. (ECB)

banking_stability.notes: High capitalisation and low NPLs, fully recovered from the 2013 crisis. (Bank of Slovenia FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A high-complexity export economy (pharma, autos, machinery), no resource curse, with deep German/Austrian supply-chain exposure. (OEC; IMAD)

institutional.notes: Strong, EU-standard institutions with a slight downward drift.

rule_of_law.notes: Independent courts and an EU-standard, high WJP standing with modest erosion noted. (WJP 2025)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Capable and EU-absorptive, with procurement/staffing flags. (TI Slovenia)

press_freedom.notes: Free (RSF ~35th), with recurring political pressure on public media. (RSF 2025/2026)

political_social.notes: A stable democracy with strong liberties, against ageing demographics.

political_stability.notes: A stable democracy that produced an orderly, lawful change of government in 2026, with high polarisation and a backsliding-risk watch under the returning right. (Balkan Insight)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong, with a watch under the new government. (WJP)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Schengen and lawful, with the historical 'erased' issue largely resolved. (EC)

social_cohesion.notes: High trust and low inequality, with some political polarisation. (Eurostat)

demographics.notes: Low fertility (~1.58), a median age ~45, and pronounced ageing - a structural drag. (SURS; Eurostat)

geopolitical.notes: A reliably Western-aligned NATO/EU member with no active conflict.

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict in a stable neighbourhood. (general)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EC)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent infrastructure, healthcare, and safety, against recurrent catastrophic flooding.

climate.notes: Transitional - alpine north plus Adriatic/Mediterranean heat-drought-wildfire and severe 2023 flooding. (EEA; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Universal single-insurer care, with co-pays abolished in 2024. (WHO Europe)

infrastructure.notes: Excellent and EU-funded, with flood-resilience upgrades underway. (EC)

crime_safety.notes: The lowest crime in the EU, with homicide ~0.5/100k. (Eurostat; GPI)

disaster_insurance.notes: A functioning flood/seismic market stress-tested by 2023, with a protection gap. (insurance press)

summary: A small, wealthy, stable EU and eurozone democracy with strong, EU-standard institutions, sound banks, a high-complexity export economy (pharma, autos, machinery), universal healthcare, and the lowest crime in the EU. The downside tails are structural rather than acute: an ageing population, heavy export concentration in German/Austrian supply chains, and recurrent catastrophic flooding (the 2023 floods alone cost ~16% of GDP). A 2026 change of government was orderly and lawful but carries a democratic-backsliding watch.
